profile.name: Jane Du
profile.headline: PhD Candidate in Computer Science at UIUC
bio: I am a PhD candidate at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign studying Computer Science. My research interests span federated learning, optimization, and differential privacy, with a current focus on personalized federated learning for multitask and multi-domain settings.

I am currently working with Professor Arindam Banerjee on multidomain and multitask learning, including federated learning. I received my BS and MS from Cornell University, with minors in Mathematics and Electrical and Computer Engineering. My MS advisors were Karthik Sridharan and Alexander Townsend.

publications.title: Computationally Efficient Methods for Invariant Feature Selection with Sparsity
publications.excerpt: Invariant Risk Minimization (IRM) proposes an optimization scheme that uses causal features to improve generalization, and this work studies sparse and computationally efficient methods for invariant feature recovery.
publications.citation: Du, J., and Banerjee, A. (2025). Computationally Efficient Methods for Invariant Feature Selection with Sparsity.

publications.title: Tracking the Hidden Trade of Non-native Pet Amphibians in the United States
publications.excerpt: This paper investigates the hidden trade of non-native pet amphibians in the United States.
publications.citation: Edmonds, D., Du, J., Stickley, S., and Sucre, S. (2026). Tracking the Hidden Trade of Non-native Pet Amphibians in the United States. Biological Conservation.

publications.title: Self-Navigating Robots Use BLE
publications.excerpt: Self-navigating robots using Bluetooth signal strength to determine distance.
publications.citation: Du, Jane and Glueck, Jacob (2018). Self-Navigating Robots Use BLE. Circuit Cellar. 1(1).
